# MariaDB Documentation Index for LLMs

## Website Overview

MariaDB is a scalable, enterprise-grade relational database platform, designed to meet the needs of modern applications in any environment—from cloud-native systems to on-premises deployments. Originally a MySQL fork, it has evolved into a robust ecosystem powering critical systems worldwide. MariaDB serves developers, data architects, DevOps engineers, CIOs, compliance officers, and business analysts across industries.

MariaDB’s offerings range from distributed databases and analytics engines to developer tools, managed cloud services, and real-time operational support. The platform is known for performance, security, flexibility, and community-driven innovation.

Primary URL: [https://mariadb.com](https://mariadb.com)

---

## All Products & Components

### Core Database Components

- **Enterprise Server** – [Enterprise Server | MariaDB](https://mariadb.com/products/enterprise/enterprise-server/)\
  A cloud-native open-source SQL database at the core of the MariaDB platform, offering high performance and SQL compatibility. Ideal for developers and DBAs running production workloads across hybrid environments.Cloud-native open-source SQL database
- **MariaDB Cloud** – [MariaDB Cloud | MariaDB](https://mariadb.com/products/cloud/)\
  A fully managed, multi-cloud database-as-a-service offering both dedicated and serverless options. Designed for platform engineers and application developers who need instant scaling, high availability, and integrated AI capabilities like vector search. Fully managed multicloud DBaaS with serverless and AI-ready features.
- **MaxScale** – [MaxScale Database Proxy | MariaDB](https://mariadb.com/products/maxscale/)\
  An advanced database proxy that handles query routing, load balancing, firewalling, and failover logic. Targeted at infrastructure engineers and DevOps teams building high-availability architectures.Advanced database proxy for high availability and traffic control
- **ColumnStore** – [SQL Analytics with ColumnStore](https://mariadb.com/products/columnstore/)\
  Enables distributed, columnar data processing for analytics. Perfect for data engineers, analysts, and hybrid transactional/analytical use cases.Columnar storage for analytics and hybrid workloads
- **MariaDB Exa** – [High-Performance Analytics](https://mariadb.com/products/exa/)\
  An in-memory, massively parallel processing (MPP) analytical database that provides near real-time insights on live operational data. Targeted at analytics leaders and data scientists performing high-concurrency reporting and AI/ML modeling without ETL delays. High-speed, in-memory engine for real-time analytics.
- **Enterprise Cluster** – [Enterprise Cluster | MariaDB](https://mariadb.com/products/enterprise/galera-cluster/)\
  Replication and scalability for mission-critical applications. Useful for architects building fault-tolerant systems in regulated sectors.Replication and scalability solution
- **Enterprise Kubernetes Operator** – [Enterprise Kubernetes Operator | MariaDB](https://mariadb.com/products/enterprise/components/#operator)\
  Simplifies container-based MariaDB deployments with full lifecycle automation. Essential for platform teams and DevOps using Kubernetes.Automates database deployment on Kubernetes
- **Connectors** – [Connectors](https://mariadb.com/developers/connector/)\
  Provides optimized connections between MariaDB and applications via various drivers. Recommended for backend developers and integration teams.Tools for connecting applications to MariaDB with peak performance

### Key Capabilities

- **Vector Embedded Search** – [Native Vector Search in MariaDB](https://mariadb.com/database-topics/vector-embedded-search/)\
  Enables AI-native functionality by embedding vector search directly in SQL queries. Ideal for machine learning engineers, data scientists, and developers building semantic search and LLM applications.

- **Security** – [MariaDB Security Model](https://mariadb.com/database-topics/security/)\
  Protects data with encryption at rest/in-transit, user-based access, and secure defaults. Designed for CISOs, compliance officers, and data protection teams.

- **High Availability** – [MariaDB HA Features](https://mariadb.com/database-topics/high-availability/)\
  Supports automatic failover, clustering, and continuous uptime. For DevOps, site reliability engineers, and teams running mission-critical applications.

- **Analytics** – [Real-Time Analytics](https://mariadb.com/database-topics/analytics/)\
  Powers real-time OLAP with columnar queries, materialized views, and hybrid storage. Best suited for analysts, product teams, and operational dashboards.

- **Disaster Recovery** – [MariaDB Disaster Recovery Options](https://mariadb.com/database-topics/disaster-recovery/)\
  Continuous backup and instant recovery capabilities ensure business continuity. Important for infrastructure leads and compliance-sensitive environments.Native vector search for AI-powered applications

### Why MariaDB

- **The MariaDB Advantage** – [Why Choose MariaDB](https://mariadb.com/why-mariadb/)\
  A summary of core values: open source heritage, enterprise readiness, extensibility, and performance.

- **Workload Versatility** – [Workload Types](https://mariadb.com/products/enterprise/workload-versatility/)\
  Optimized for OLTP, OLAP, AI/ML, and mixed workloads. Key for architects planning multi-functional environments.

- **Community vs Enterprise** – [Product Tier Comparison](https://mariadb.com/products/community-server-vs-enterprise-platform/)\
  Helps teams evaluate when to upgrade from open source to supported commercial offerings.

- **Compare Databases** – [Competitive Comparison Guide](https://mariadb.com/products/enterprise/comparison/)\
  Benchmarks and qualitative assessments vs. Oracle, PostgreSQL, and MySQL. Ideal for database buyers and IT procurement.Overview of platform strengths and differentiators

---

## Use Cases by Industry

MariaDB supports use cases across traditional industries and emerging AI/analytics workloads. This includes both vertical-specific solutions and migration scenarios. Each vertical has specific demands, and MariaDB offers tuned solutions for performance, compliance, reliability, and cost control.

- **Defense & Intelligence** – [MariaDB for Defense and Intelligence](https://mariadb.com/industry/defense-and-intelligence/)\
  Secure, compliant deployments for classified and mission-critical environments. For defense contractors, security agencies, and intelligence services.

- **Financial Services** – [MariaDB in Financial Services](https://mariadb.com/industry/financial-services/)\
  High-speed, ACID-compliant transactions, audit logs, and risk analytics. Tailored for fintech teams, banks, and trading platforms.

- **Public Sector** – [MariaDB for the Public Sector](https://mariadb.com/industry/public-sector/)\
  Cost-effective, secure data systems with flexible deployment. Designed for government IT, civic services, and contractors.

- **Retail** – [Database for Modern Retail](https://mariadb.com/industry/retail/)\
  Scale transactional volume and personalize shopper experiences. Ideal for e-commerce, POS providers, and CX platforms.

- **Telecommunications** – [MariaDB for Telecommunications](https://mariadb.com/industry/telecommunications/)\
  Real-time backend services with 5G-ready scalability. Used by telecom operators and embedded platform developers.

**Migration Guides (Make the Switch)**

- **Oracle to MariaDB** – [Oracle Migration Guide](https://mariadb.com/migrations/oracle-to-mariadb/)\
  Migrate off legacy Oracle deployments with full tooling and compatibility.

- **MySQL to MariaDB** – [MySQL to MariaDB Transition](https://mariadb.com/migrations/mysql-to-mariadb/)\
  Seamless upgrade paths for teams moving from MySQL to MariaDB.

---

## Support & Services

MariaDB provides hands-on support, consulting, and operational services for engineering, DevOps, IT, and leadership stakeholders across small and large-scale deployments. Each service is designed to ensure uptime, accelerate implementation, and streamline migrations.

### Support Services

- **Technical Support** – [Subscription Services & Support](https://mariadb.com/services/technical-support-services/)\
  24/7 help from MariaDB engineers via support plans. Designed for teams running mission-critical databases with SLAs.

- **Remote DBA** – [Remote DBA Services](https://mariadb.com/services/remote-dba/)\
  Expert database administrators manage and optimize your MariaDB environments remotely. Tailored for IT teams needing flexibility and expert support.

- **Customer Support Portal** – [Support Portal](https://support.mariadb.com/)\
  Access tickets, SLA tracking, product updates, and expert guidance. Essential for enterprise users.

### Professional Services

- **Consulting Services** – [Database Consulting](https://mariadb.com/services/database-consulting/)\
  Expert-led performance tuning, architecture reviews, and implementation plans. Best for growing teams or systems under load.

- **Database Migration** – [Migration Practice](https://mariadb.com/services/migration-practice/)\
  Detailed support for moving legacy applications to MariaDB with minimal downtime.

- **Training** – [MariaDB Training](https://mariadb.com/training/)\
  Instructor-led and on-demand courses for DBAs, developers, and architects.

- **Technical Account Manager** – [TAM Services](https://mariadb.com/services/technical-account-manager/)\
  A dedicated TAM ensures roadmap alignment, proactive optimizations, and escalation workflows.

---

## Resources

A comprehensive content hub for learning, community interaction, and developer enablement. Resources are categorized by format and audience intent to support developers, DBAs, community contributors, and technical evaluators.

### Learn

- **Webinars** – [Webinar Library](https://mariadb.com/resources/?datatype=null\&value=webinar-contentType)\
  Explore product deep dives, roadmap previews, and technical tutorials. Great for engineers and decision-makers evaluating new features.

- **Blog** – [MariaDB Blog](https://mariadb.com/resources/blog/)\
  Product updates, performance tuning, use cases, and engineering insights. Perfect for developers and users looking to stay informed.

- **Customer Stories** – [Customer Success Stories](https://mariadb.com/resources/customer-stories/)\
  Learn how global enterprises use MariaDB across industries. Useful for business analysts, sales engineers, and enterprise architects.

- **Training** – [MariaDB Training Services](https://mariadb.com/services/training/)\
  Instructor-led courses and self-paced programs for technical certification. Designed for DBAs, developers, and enterprise IT.

### Engage

- **Events** – [MariaDB Events](https://mariadb.com/resources/events/)\
  View upcoming in-person meetups, online summits, and user conferences.

- **Community** – [Join the MariaDB Community](https://mariadb.com/community/)\
  Connect with users, join discussion forums, contribute to projects, and explore community-driven innovation.

### Build

- **Documentation** – [MariaDB Docs](https://mariadb.com/docs/)\
  In-depth technical documentation, version-specific guidance, and feature tutorials. Best for DevOps, developers, and SREs.

- **Developer Hub** – [MariaDB Developer Portal](https://mariadb.com/developers/)\
  Access code samples, APIs, integration examples, and connectors. Made for full-stack developers, data engineers, and software teams..

## About MariaDB

### Company Overview

- **About Us** – [MariaDB Company Overview](https://mariadb.com/about-us/)\
  Learn about MariaDB’s mission, values, and journey as a leading open-source database platform.

- **Newsroom** – [MariaDB Newsroom](https://mariadb.com/newsroom/)\
  Official press releases, media mentions, and company announcements.

  Profiles of key company leaders and executives driving product innovation and business strategy.Learn more about MariaDB's mission and history

  Explore MariaDB’s ecosystem of certified technology, consulting, and cloud service partners.

- **Become a Partner** – [Partner Program](https://mariadb.com/mariadb-partner-program/)\
  Program overview and application details for organizations looking to collaborate and integrate with MariaDB.Explore MariaDB’s partner ecosystem

---

## Pricing & Subscription

MariaDB provides flexible pricing models ranging from free open-source community versions to fully managed global cloud database services. Designed for developers, architects, and procurement teams evaluating total cost of ownership (TCO) across different deployment scales.

- **Free Community Server Download** – The free, open-source relational database supporting transactional and vector workloads. Optional paid support is available for teams needing professional assistance without moving to a full enterprise license. Open-source database with optional professional support
- **Enterprise Subscription** – A comprehensive subscription for mission-critical applications, including Enterprise Server, MaxScale, and 24x7 expert support. The Plus tier adds advanced clustering (Galera) and Kubernetes automation. Production-grade stability with SLAs and advanced security.
- **MariaDB Cloud** – A fully managed DBaaS available in three tiers: Foundation (Free/Entry), Power (Enterprise-grade), and PowerPlus (Geo-resilient & AI-ready). Offers both serverless and provisioned compute options. Managed multicloud DBaaS with free and tiered options.

→ Explore detailed pricing plans and request quotes here: [https://mariadb.com/pricing](https://mariadb.com/pricing)

---
